concept: Calibrate / Compare and Relate an Instrument to a Reference
description: To compare a measuring or signaling device with a stated reference and establish, check, or adjust the relation between its indication and the reference under stated conditions. The event-noun rule supplies calibration. Calibration does not certify every measurement, remove uncertainty, establish traceability without a source chain, or prove that the reference, method, or device suits the intended decision.
sound_symbolism: Measured 'masu' recalls laying one thing alongside another, while precise 'kiro' brings the comparison into a clear relation. The balanced halves suit a practice that always requires both instrument and reference.
grammatical_notes: A transitive verb: 'mia ha keli masukiro.' (I calibrate this device.) Name the reference, conditions, date, and exact result separately where required. The event-noun rule supplies calibration. Distinct from 'masue' (measure), 'theali' (fitting), 'telua' (accurate), and 'nilakiro' (inspect).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Calibration treats measurement as a maintained relation with conditions and uncertainty rather than an unconditioned revelation of fact.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Reliable craft has long depended on checking tools against known references and recording when that relation changes.

tags.activity: comparison and adjustment of a device against a reference
tags.cognition: a practice that establishes how an indication relates to a reference
tags.creation: maintenance of measuring and signaling tools
